title: Grilled Corn and Seafood Recipes for Summer
meta_description: Grilled corn and seafood recipes for summer, plus a nifty gadget to keep your corn intact. Perfect for BBQs and parties!
html:
Summer means backyard barbecues, and grilled corn and seafood recipes are a match made in heat. But if you’ve ever tried grilling corn without it slipping off the grill, you know it’s a pain. Enter the Girla Interlocking Corn on the Cob Holders—a game-changer for keeping your corn secure while you char it to perfection. Pair it with seafood, and you’ve got a summer spread that’s juicy, smoky, and totally stress-free.

Why Grilled Corn and Seafood Are a Summer Perfect Pair

Grilled corn’s sweet, charred flavor balances seafood’s briny, buttery notes. Think of it like this: corn’s crunch contrasts with tender shrimp or scallops, and both soak up smoky grill flavors. Plus, they cook in similar times, making them easy to prep together. Whether you’re tossing them into a salad, stacking into tacos, or grilling on skewers, the combo is summer’s ultimate comfort food.

Essential Tips for Grilling Corn and Seafood Like a Pro

1. **Prep corn properly**: Remove silk and leaves, then soak in water for 15-20 minutes to prevent burning. The Girla Corn Holders let you skip wrapping in foil—just slide them on and grill straight.

2. **Oil the grill, not the food**: Brush grates with oil, not the corn or seafood, to avoid flare-ups. Direct heat gives that perfect char.

3. **Seafood tricks**: Shrimp cook fast—keep them on 2-3 minutes per side. For salmon or fish, wrap in parchment with herbs for a moist finish.

3 Grilled Corn and Seafood Recipes to Try This Summer

1. Grilled Corn and Shrimp Skewers

  • Slip corn onto Girla Holders and brush with butter.
  • Thread shrimp onto skewers with garlic, lime, and chili.
  • Grill corn 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally; shrimp need 2-3 minutes per side.

2. Seafood Corn Salad

Combine grilled corn, chopped shrimp, avocado, and red onion. Toss with lime juice, cilantro, and a drizzle of olive oil. Serve chilled.

3. Grilled Corn and Fish Tacos

Place white fish (like tilapia) and corn onto Girla Holders. Brush with taco seasoning. Warm tortillas and top with slaw and lime crema.

How the Girla Holders Make Grilling Easier (and Cuter)

The Girla Interlocking Corn Holders solve two problems: they keep corn from twisting off the grill and store neatly in their interlocking design. The silicone handles stay cool to the touch, so you won’t burn your hands. They’re also dishwasher-safe—a win for post-party cleanup. Use them for veggies too, like zucchini or mushrooms.

Serving Suggestions for Your Summer Spread

Pair grilled corn and seafood with zesty sides like quinoa, coleslaw, or grilled bread. For drinks, go light—lemonade or a crisp white wine. If you’re hosting, these recipes double easily for crowds. The corn holders’s bright colors even add a festive touch to your table!

FAQ

Can I use the corn holders on a gas or charcoal grill?

Absolutely! They work on any heat source where you’d grill corn—just avoid direct flame on the silicone handles.

How do I store them when not in use?

The interlocking design lets you stack them in a drawer or on a shelf without taking up much space.

Are they safe for cleaning?

Yes, they’re dishwasher-safe and top-rack friendly. No need to soak—they wipe clean easily too.
